title: docker-compose build fails to build local image
body:
### Issue Description

When a Dockerfile references an image local to the system, it builds fine with podman, but fails to build when using compose and podman.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. `podman pull quay.io/podman/hello`
2. `podman tag quay.io/podman/hello hello-test`
3. Dockerfile
```
FROM hello-test
```
4. docker-compose.yml
```
version: '3'
services:
    hello-world:
        build:
                context: .
                dockerfile: Dockerfile
```
4.
```
# docker-compose build
[+] Building 0.2s (2/2) FINISHED                                                            docker-container:default
 => [hello-world internal] load build definition from Dockerfile                                                0.0s
 => => transferring dockerfile: 113B                                                                            0.0s
 => ERROR [hello-world internal] load metadata for docker.io/library/hello-test:latest                          0.2s
------
 > [hello-world internal] load metadata for docker.io/library/hello-test:latest:
------
failed to solve: hello-test: pull access denied, repository does not exist or may require authorization: server message: insufficient_scope: authorization failed
```


### Describe the results you received

The image fails to build, it seems to be looking at docker.io instead of localhost

The image exists locally, and builds fine when using standalone podman:

```
# podman images
REPOSITORY               TAG              IMAGE ID      CREATED       SIZE
quay.io/podman/hello     latest           e2b3db5d4fdf  4 months ago  83.1 kB
localhost/hello-test     latest           e2b3db5d4fdf  4 months ago  83.1 kB
```

And building standalone:
```
# podman build .
STEP 1/1: FROM hello-test
COMMIT
--> e2b3db5d4fdf
e2b3db5d4fdf670b56dd7138d53b5974f2893a965f7d37486fbb9fcbf5e91d9d
```

### Describe the results you expected

I would expect compose to allow building of the same Dockerfile as podman is able to.

This exact same setup works fine with Docker.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

# docker-compose version
Docker Compose version v2.23.3

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: Issue was compose trying to use buildkit, fix was putting `DOCKER_BUILDKIT=0` before the command.
